title: useIdle hook to detect user inactivity for auto-collapse UI
scenario_context: Video player hides controls after 3s of no interaction. Hook detects idle state via mouse move + key + touch.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Inactivity detected across all input types.\n2. Reset timer on any input.\n3. Single hook for all consumers.

detection_strategy.code_pattern: events fire → reset timer; timer fires → idle=true

accessibility_across_viewports: Keyboard activity resets — keyboard users not penalized.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Per-consumer setup leads to duplicate listeners.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) All input types. 2) Centralized timer. 3) Configurable.

title: Author byline that shows compact or expanded based on container
scenario_context: 'By Jane Doe · Editor · 5 min read'. Compact: 'Jane Doe'. Container width drives.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Reveal more author info as space allows.\n2. Container queries.\n3. Progressive disclosure.
accessibility_across_viewports: All info in DOM (screen readers see all).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Truncating with ... loses meaning.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Container-driven. 2) Progressive. 3) Always essential.

title: Dropcap on articles that scales with paragraph text
scenario_context: First letter of article styled as dropcap. Should scale with paragraph font size fluidly.
responsive_reasoning: 1. ::first-letter pseudo for dropcap.\n2. em-based size scales with text.\n3. float: left for wrap.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Fixed px makes drop look wrong at extremes.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) em-based. 2) Pseudo-element. 3) Editorial polish.

title: Center-aligned content with min margin guarantee
scenario_context: Container centered with max-width 1200px. Should have minimum 1rem padding from viewport edges always.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Content touching edges at narrow viewports
max-width with margin: auto only (no padding)
responsive_reasoning: 1. max-width centers.\n2. Padding ensures edge gap.\n3. min() prevents excess on wide.
what_breaks_naive_approach: max-width without padding has content touching edges.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Min padding guarantee. 2) Fluid scaling. 3) Universal pattern.

title: Right-click + long-press to open same context menu via library or custom
scenario_context: Generic context menu pattern handling right-click (mouse) and long-press (touch) consistently.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Both gestures trigger same menu.\n2. Per responsive-095.\n3. Menu adapts position.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Touch-only excludes desktop right-click users.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Three triggers. 2) Same menu. 3) WAI-ARIA.

title: Username/password autofill that respects iOS Safari quirks
scenario_context: iOS Safari's password manager fills via tap above keyboard. Form must have proper autocomplete tokens.
responsive_reasoning: 1. autoComplete tokens enable autofill.\n2. Apple Passkeys use same tokens.\n3. inputMode for keyboard.
accessibility_across_viewports: Standard tokens work with all assistive tech.
what_breaks_naive_approach: autoComplete='off' breaks password managers — users have to type manually.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Standard tokens. 2) Universal autofill. 3) Apple Passkey support.

title: Header that's transparent over content with safe area for status bar
scenario_context: Hero page with transparent header overlay. Status bar text needs contrast (white text on dark hero).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No safe area = text under status bar
responsive_reasoning: 1. Header is transparent over hero.\n2. Padding for status bar.\n3. Background appears on scroll.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Scroll past hero — header becomes opaque

performance_notes: Single listener. Transition for smooth swap.
edge_cases_handled: Top of page — transparent over hero
Safe area inset for status bar
what_breaks_naive_approach: No safe area means text behind status bar.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Scroll-aware. 2) Safe area. 3) iOS-native feel.

title: Live broadcast layout: streamer video + chat + viewer count adaptive
scenario_context: Live stream view. Portrait phone: video on top, chat below. Landscape: video centered, chat sidebar.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Orientation drives layout split.\n2. Portrait stacks. Landscape side-by-side.\n3. Video always prominent.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Portrait: scroll between. Landscape: dual view.

accessibility_across_viewports: Real video element. Chat is aside.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Same layout wastes one orientation's affordances.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Orientation-driven. 2) Aspect-locked video. 3) Same data.

title: Drag and drop between apps via Web Share Target API
scenario_context: Web app declares itself as Share Target. Users can share content from other apps directly into web app.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Web Share Target API enables cross-app receipt.\n2. Manifest declaration.\n3. URL handler for shared content.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Share from app → web app receives

what_breaks_naive_approach: No cross-app share = users manually copy/paste.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Web Share Target. 2) Cross-app. 3) PWA enhancement.

title: Search results layout with filters: sidebar desktop, drawer mobile
scenario_context: Search results with category, price, brand filters. Desktop: sidebar filters. Mobile: filter button → drawer.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Filters inline above results on mobile
responsive_reasoning: 1. Filters supplemental to results.\n2. Desktop sidebar always visible.\n3. Mobile drawer on demand.
accessibility_across_viewports: aside landmark. Drawer role=dialog when open.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Sidebar on tablet cramped. Inline filters push results.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Drawer vs sidebar. 2) Same filters. 3) Results primary.

title: Table cell that supports inline rich editing with formatting toolbar
scenario_context: Cells with rich text. Inline edit on desktop. Mobile: tap → fullscreen editor with toolbar.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Toolbar appears near focused cell.\n2. Mobile sheet for clarity.\n3. Same data model.

interaction_model_per_device.desktop_mouse: Click → inline edit with floating toolbar

what_breaks_naive_approach: Toolbar covers row above on small cells.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Adaptive UI. 2) Toolbar placement. 3) Mobile clarity.

title: Drag-to-reorder list inside form (saved order)
scenario_context: Reorder items in form (e.g., notification priorities). Touch and mouse and keyboard.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Per responsive-020 + form context.\n2. Saved order on form submit.\n3. Universal pattern.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Touch-only drag in form locks out keyboard users.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Universal reorder. 2) Form context. 3) State on submit.

title: Image gallery zoom transition: thumbnail to lightbox with shared element animation
scenario_context: Tap thumbnail, image animates expanding to fullscreen. Reverse on close. Shared element transition for spatial continuity.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Shared element transition with FLIP technique.\n2. Respects reduced motion.\n3. GPU-accelerated.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: FLIP animation between thumbnail and full

performance_notes: View Transitions API native (Chrome). Skip on reduced motion.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Heavy JS animation kills low-end devices.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Native View Transitions. 2) Reduced motion. 3) Fallback to instant.

title: Sticky table-of-contents on long article with scroll-spy
scenario_context: Article TOC stays visible as user scrolls. Highlights current section.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Per responsive-164.\n2. Sticky TOC on desktop, button on mobile.\n3. IntersectionObserver for spy.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Static TOC scrolls out of view.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Sticky + spy. 2) Adaptive presentation. 3) Anchor nav.

title: Snackbar queue: multiple toasts stack with auto-dismiss timing
scenario_context: Multiple toasts can appear. Queue manages display, prevents overlap, auto-dismisses.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Queue with priorities.\n2. Stagger dismissals.\n3. Limit visible count.
performance_notes: Array limited to max. Stagger via setTimeout per toast.
what_breaks_naive_approach: All toasts at once overflow screen.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Queue management. 2) Max visible. 3) Auto-dismiss timing.

title: Performance budget for mobile: bundle <100KB, image <500KB total
scenario_context: Performance budget enforced. Bundle small. Images optimized. Critical CSS inlined.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Budget per route.\n2. Code splitting.\n3. Image optimization.
accessibility_across_viewports: Faster app = better for all users, especially screen reader users on slow devices.
edge_cases_handled: Low-end CPU — small JS to parse
what_breaks_naive_approach: 500KB bundle delays interactive by 5+ seconds on slow networks.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Performance budget. 2) Code splitting. 3) Image optimization.

title: Hook to detect when user scrolled past a threshold (e.g., 50% of page)
scenario_context: Track if user scrolled past 50% of article for analytics. Single threshold detection.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Single threshold check.\n2. Fire once.\n3. rAF throttle.
edge_cases_handled: Short pages (no scroll) — threshold check guards
what_breaks_naive_approach: Per-scroll setState burns CPU.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) rAF throttle. 2) Auto-cleanup. 3) Universal pattern.

title: Notification card with icon, content, action that adapts container
scenario_context: Notification card. Icon + content + action button. Wide: inline. Narrow: stacked.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Container queries decide arrangement.\n2. Wide: row. Narrow: column.\n3. Action always visible.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Forced inline cramps narrow.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Container arrangement. 2) Action always visible. 3) Universal.

title: Math equations (KaTeX/MathJax) scaling with text size
scenario_context: Math formulas rendered via KaTeX. Should scale with surrounding text via em units.
responsive_reasoning: 1. KaTeX outputs em-relative sizes.\n2. Inherits parent font size.\n3. Fluid scale flows through.
accessibility_across_viewports: KaTeX renders MathML for screen readers.
edge_cases_handled: Mobile — math scales with body text
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) em-relative. 2) Inherits fluid type. 3) Horizontal scroll if needed.

title: Form field gap that adapts to error message presence
scenario_context: Form fields normally have consistent gap. When error appears, more space needed. Layout shouldn't shift other fields.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Reserve space for error.\n2. min-height on error area.\n3. No CLS.
accessibility_across_viewports: role=alert when error appears. Reserved height prevents CLS.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Conditional error pushes layout.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Reserved space. 2) No CLS. 3) Live region.

title: Scroll-snap carousel that respects keyboard arrow navigation
scenario_context: Carousel that snaps. Keyboard users press arrow to scroll to next item. Scroll-snap maintains alignment.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Native scroll-snap CSS.\n2. Arrow keys natively scroll.\n3. Snap aligns to nearest.
accessibility_across_viewports: Focusable items. Native arrow scroll. snap aligns.
edge_cases_handled: Keyboard arrows — focus next then auto-scroll snaps
what_breaks_naive_approach: Mandatory snap without focusable items skips arrow nav.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Native snap. 2) Focusable items. 3) Universal nav.

title: Input that prevents zoom on iOS when font-size is below 16px
scenario_context: iOS Safari zooms in on focused input if font-size < 16px. Ensure inputs use 16px+ to prevent zoom.
responsive_reasoning: 1. iOS zooms inputs <16px.\n2. Use text-base (16px) on inputs.\n3. Don't disable zoom (a11y).

detection_strategy.code_pattern: input { font-size: 16px; } /* or 1rem if base is 16 */

edge_cases_handled: Custom font sizes — keep input at 16+
Android — no zoom anyway, but consistent
what_breaks_naive_approach: text-sm on inputs triggers iOS zoom, disorienting.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) 16px minimum. 2) No zoom disable. 3) iOS UX preserved.

title: Game UI elements positioned at safe corners
scenario_context: Game HUD elements (score, lives, settings) in corners. Must respect safe areas on phones in landscape.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Corner elements need safe area.\n2. Different inset per corner.\n3. env() per direction.
edge_cases_handled: Landscape iPhone — corners respect notch + indicator
what_breaks_naive_approach: Hardcoded positions overlap notch.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Per-corner safe. 2) max() floor. 3) Universal.

title: Settings page that shows sections side-by-side in landscape on tablet
scenario_context: Settings page with categories. Portrait: list view. Landscape: master-detail (category list + details).
responsive_reasoning: 1. Per responsive-009 tablet split-pane.\n2. Settings is great fit for master-detail.\n3. Orientation drives.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Master-detail in landscape. 2) Orientation aware. 3) Tablet optimization.

title: iPad-specific drag and drop within app for reorganization
scenario_context: iPad supports rich drag and drop in apps. Custom items draggable to reorder, drop on targets.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Per responsive-020 patterns with iPad enhancement.\n2. dnd-kit handles both.\n3. Native iPad drag uses TouchEvent.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Pointer events unify. 2) iPad-native. 3) Keyboard alt.

title: App with floating panel that can be dragged to any corner
scenario_context: Helper panel (chat assistant, music player) floats above content. User can drag to any corner. Persists position.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Panel position user-controlled.\n2. Snap to corners.\n3. Safe area aware.
accessibility_across_viewports: Position persisted; users with preferences honored.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Fixed position doesn't respect user preference.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Corner snap. 2) Safe area. 3) Persistence.

title: Excel-style cell selection range with keyboard shift+arrow
scenario_context: Click cell to select. Shift+arrow extends range. Keyboard nav between cells.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Click to select.\n2. Shift+click for range.\n3. Arrow keys nav, Shift+arrow extend.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap cell, drag corner to extend

what_breaks_naive_approach: Mouse-only locks out keyboard.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Universal selection. 2) Range support. 3) Keyboard accessible.

title: Inline form validation with debounced async validation
scenario_context: Username field checks availability via API. Debounce to avoid spam.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Debounce (500ms typical).\n2. Loading state during check.\n3. Result displayed.
edge_cases_handled: Type fast — only final value checked
Network error — falls back to idle
Min length — no check below 3 chars
what_breaks_naive_approach: No debounce spams API. No visual feedback confuses user.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Debounced. 2) Live region. 3) Visual status.

title: WebP/AVIF support detection with fallback to JPEG
scenario_context: Modern formats reduce file size 30-50%. Browser support varies. Use picture element with type fallback.
responsive_reasoning: 1. picture+source with type attribute.\n2. Browser picks first supported.\n3. JPEG ultimate fallback.
edge_cases_handled: Safari — picks WebP (or AVIF in newer)
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single format means larger files for everyone.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Format negotiation. 2) Universal fallback. 3) Native browser pick.

title: Pagination using Next/Prev buttons with keyboard shortcuts
scenario_context: Article series with Next/Prev page links. Keyboard shortcuts (J/K or Arrow keys) navigate.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Only button click (slow for power users)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Next/Prev links visible.\n2. Keyboard shortcuts for power users.\n3. Title alt text describes target.
what_breaks_naive_approach: No keyboard shortcuts slow power users.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Real links. 2) Keyboard shortcuts. 3) Title preview.
